zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sybill de Sancta Maria于大约1130年出生在英国越可爾阿爾德瓦可时，她的父亲Paganus de Sancta Maria36岁，母亲Mrs Paganus Sancta Maria34岁。她于1147年与Radulphus de Clarell在英国英格兰越可爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sancta
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Gerardo, Juan, Roberto, Aida, Alberto, Alicia, Angelina, Blanca, Carlos, Cesar.

Spanish and Portuguese: nickname from santa ‘(female) saint’, or a habitational name from any of various minor places called Santa, or a topographic name denoting someone who lived near a sanctuary. Compare Spanish Lasanta .
Hungarian (Sánta): descriptive nickname for a disabled person, from sánta ‘lame’.

variants.name: Lasanta
meanings: Spanish: variant of Santa ‘she-saint’, with fused definite article la.

nameMeanings.name: Maria
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Ana, Elena, Manuel, Pedro, Juan, Miguel, Rafael, Santa, Alejandro, Amado, Amandio.  Portuguese Joaquim.  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Salvatore, Albertina, Antonino, Cataldo, Filiberto, Ignazio, Plinio, Sal.

Spanish (María); Italian and Portuguese; Hungarian (Mária): from the female personal name, Latin Maria. This was the name of the mother of Jesus Christ in the New Testament, as well as several other New Testament figures. The Latin form of the name, on which all common variants from European languages are based (including English Mary ), derived as a back-formation from the early Christian female name Mariam. This was mistaken for an accusative case, with the usual Latin feminine accusative ending -am, but in fact it is an Aramaic form (originally Maryam) of the Hebrew name Miryam. The Hebrew name is of uncertain etymology, perhaps from a word meaning ‘wished-for child’, from an Egyptian root mrj with the addition of the Hebrew feminine diminutive suffix -am. Saint Jerome understood it as a compound of mar ‘drop’ + yam ‘sea’, which he rendered as Latin stilla maris, later altered to stella maris ‘star of the sea’, whence the medieval Christian liturgical phrase.
French: Latinized form of   Marie  , a cognate of 1 above. It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin.

variants.name: Marie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jacques, Cecile, Alain, Benoit, Camille, Edouard, Francoise, Jean Jacques, Jean-Philippe, Stephane.
French and English (Jersey; also Surrey and Middlesex): from the female personal name Marie, Latin Maria . In England it is in part of Huguenot origin. Compare Mary .

French (Marié): from marié ‘married’, the past participle of marier ‘to marry’, a nickname for a man newly married or perhaps for a son-in-law, or a status name for a married individual used to distinguish him from an unmarried namesake. Compare Lemarie and Marier .

variants.name: Marietta
meanings: Italian: from a pet form of the female personal name   Maria  .

variants.name: Maric
meanings: Serbian and Croatian (Marić); Slovenian (Marič): metronymic from the female personal name Marija (see Maria ), or its short form Mara. Marić is the fourth most frequent surname in Croatia.

variants.name: Marien
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand.
Flemish, German, and Dutch: metronymic from the personal name   Maria  .
German: topographic name for someone who lived by a church named for one of the Marys of the New Testament: Mary the Mother of Jesus, Mary Magdalene, or Mary sister of Martha.

variants.name: Mares
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Jesus, Luis, Mario, Ruben, Arturo, Guadalupe, Rafael, Ramon.
Catalan (Marès, also Marés): topographic name from Catalan marès ‘by the sea’.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Le Marais-la-Chapelle (Calvados), Marais-Vernier (Eure), or various other minor places in northern France named with Old French marais, Norman-Picard marese ‘marsh’.

variants.name: Marinko
meanings: Slovenian, Croatian, Serbian, and Slovak:
metronymic from the personal name Marija (see Maria ).
from the personal name Marinko, a pet form of Marin 1 or a male derivative of the female personal name Marija.

variants.name: Mariona
meanings: Catalan: from the female personal name Mariona, a pet form of Maria . This surname is very rare in Spain; it is found mainly in El Salvador.

variants.name: Manak
meanings: Czech (Maňák): metronymic from the female personal name Máňa, a pet form of Marie (see Maria ). It may also denoted the husband of a woman named Máňa.

variants.name: Mario
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Angelo, Carmine, Giovanna, Natale, Sal, Vito.  Spanish Jose, Alfonso, Fernandez, Garcia, Jesus, Jorge, Mario, Martinez, Ortiz, Paz, Rodriguez, Rosalina.

Italian, Spanish, and Portuguese: from the personal name Mario, from the Latin name Marius, which is probably of Etruscan origin and possibly related to the name of the Roman god Mars, but was widely adopted from an early date in the Christian era as a male equivalent of the female Christian name Maria .

variants.name: Mary
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Myrtha, Pierre.

English: from the Middle English and Old French female personal name Marie, Latin Maria . Compare Marrison , Marion , Marriott , Markin . — Note: It is likely that in the population figure published by the US Census Bureau (which dropped from 2215 in the year 2000 to 1547 in the year 2010) the female personal name Mary of some American bearers is also counted as a surname. Some of these bearers may be from countries, such as Indonesia, where (hereditary) surnames are not in general use; their personal name was thus registered as a surname only after their immigration to the US.
French: from a variant of the female personal name   Marie  .

nameMeanings.name: Sybill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek. See Sybil .

variants.name: Sybil
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Sybill de Sancta Maria was born about 1130, in Aldwark, Yorkshire, England, her father, Paganus de Sancta Maria, was 36 and her mother, Mrs Paganus Sancta Maria, was 34. She married Radulphus de Clarell in 1147, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sybill de Sancta Maria est née environ 1130, à Aldwark, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Paganus de Sancta Maria, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Mrs Paganus Sancta Maria, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Radulphus de Clarell en 1147, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sybill de Sancta Maria um 1130 in Aldwark, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Paganus de Sancta Maria 36 und ihre Mutter Mrs Paganus Sancta Maria 34. Sie hat 1147 in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Radulphus de Clarell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sybill de Sancta Maria è nata il circa 1130 nel Aldwark, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Paganus de Sancta Maria, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Mrs Paganus Sancta Maria, aveva 34 anni. Lei sposato Radulphus de Clarell nel 1147, nel Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Sybill de Sancta Mariaが約1130年にイギリスヨークシュアイアアルドワークで生まれた時，父Paganus de Sancta Mariaは36歳，母Mrs Paganus Sancta Mariaは34歳であった。1147年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアでRadulphus de Clarellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Sybill de Sancta Maria(이)가 대략 1130년 영국 요르크실르 알드워크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Paganus de Sancta Maria(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs Paganus Sancta Maria(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1147년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 Radulphus de Clarell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sybill de Sancta Maria nasceu aproximadamente 1130, em Aldwark, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Paganus de Sancta Maria, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Mrs Paganus Sancta Maria, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com Radulphus de Clarell em 1147, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sybill de Sancta Maria родилась около 1130 г. в местности алдварк, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Paganus de Sancta Maria было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs Paganus Sancta Maria, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Radulphus de Clarell в 1147 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sybill de Sancta Maria nació aproximadamente 1130, en Aldwark, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Paganus de Sancta Maria, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Mrs Paganus Sancta Maria, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Radulphus de Clarell en 1147, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija.
